concerns.text: FR-007 and US-2 require a sensitivity sweep across thresholds {0.01, 0.05, 0.10} reporting variation in BOTH Sampling AND Specification Stability rates. Task T028 ('Implement sensitivity sweep logic') lacks explicit instruction to calculate/store the Specification Stability Rate at these thresholds, leaving FR-007's dual-metric requirement unverified.

FR-002 and Constitution Principle VI mandate 1,000 bootstrap iterations for baseline and 1,000 for each of 5 alternative specs. Tasks T022-T029 imply 500 iterations for alternatives (via T012 test description) and lack a task to dynamically configure the baseline count to 1,000, risking violation of SC-001 statistical power.

FR-004 requires recording 'Sampling Stability Rate' and 'Specification Stability Rate' for every study. Task T029 saves results to JSON but does not explicitly mandate the schema to include the proportion of iterations yielding p < 0.05 for EACH of the 5 alternative model specifications, risking an output format incompatible with US-3 aggregation.

FR-001 requires downloading data for 'at least 50 pre-registered studies'. Tasks T014-T018 cover ingestion logic but lack a specific task to implement the filtering logic for the 3 distinct disciplines (psychology, economics, biology) required by FR-001 and Constitution Principle VII.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 requires flagging entries as 'ambiguous_model' when the statistical model is described in text but lacks a numeric p-value. Task T016 flags 'missing_p_value' and 'ambiguous_model', but T015 (metadata parsing) does not explicitly include logic to parse text descriptions to detect this condition, creating a gap in the implementation path for this acceptance scenario.
